title: Vertical flight aircraft with improved stability
abstract: Devices and systems of the inventive concept provide a durable, all-weather manned or unmanned aircraft that is capable of vertical flight and provides improved stability upon payload launch or delivery. The payload bay is positioned along the central axis of the aircraft and proximal to the aircrafts center of gravity. Control and fuel systems are positioned fore and aft of the payload bay, respectively. The payload bay is configured to store and deliver a wide variety of payload types. The aircraft also includes features that reduce vibration, prolong the interval between necessary maintenance, and permit all-weather operation.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A vertical flight capable aircraft comprising: a fuselage having a fuselage length; and a payload bay positioned within about 10% of the fuselage length of the vertical flight capable aircraftâs center of gravity, wherein deployment of a payload from the payload bay results in a shift in position of the vertical flight capable aircraftâs center of gravity of less than 4% of the fuselage length. 2. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 1, wherein the payload bay is configured to store a plurality of weapon types. 3. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 1 or 2, further comprising a quad landing gear, wherein the quad landing gear is retractable into the fuselage. 4. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the payload bay comprises bay doors positioned beneath the vertical flight capable aircraft, and further comprises a gravity drop mechanism for release of payload through the bay doors. 5. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising a weapon launcher coupled to a side of the fuselage. 6. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 5, further comprising a weapon launcher configured to launch a weapon from a door of the vertical flight capable aircraft. 7. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 6, further comprising an avionics assembly positioned forward of the payload bay. 8. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 7, further comprising an infrared suppressor positioned aft of the payload bay. 9. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 8, wherein the infrared suppressor comprises an upward directed exhaust. 10. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 9, further comprising a single engine coupled to the fuselage. 11. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 10, wherein the single engine has an output of up to 1300 horsepower. 12. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 11, further comprising two or more engines coupled to a wing of the vertical flight capable aircraft. 13. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 12, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft has two engines, and in which each one of the two engines has an output of 700 to 1300 horsepower. 14. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 13, further comprising an OSR rotor. 15. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 14, further comprising an OSTR lift and propulsion mechanism. 16. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 15, further comprising a Butterfly wing flap. 17. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft is a rotorcraft. 18. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft is a winged rotorcraft. 19. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the vertical flight capable is a tilt wing aircraft. 20. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the vertical flight capable is a tilt rotor aircraft. 21. The vertical flight capable of one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft is unmanned. 22. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 21, where the avionics assembly is modular or removable. 23. The vertical flight capable of one of claims 1 to 22, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft comprises one or more of an electro -thermally heated portion of aircraft skin, a lighting- strike protection feature, an engine air particle separator, an abrasion-resistant surface coating, and a sensor suite, and is capable of flight in all-weather conditions. 24. The vertical flight capable of one of claims 1 to 23, wherein the vertical flight capable aircraft comprises one or more of an all-electric architecture, a higher-harmonic blade pitch control system, a vibration mitigation system, a rigid rotor system, a plurality of modular mission systems, a low-maintenance engine air particle separator, and an abrasion-resistant surface coating, and is capable of long periods of maintenance free operation. 25. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 24, wherein the payload bay comprises payload bay doors positioned beneath the vertical flight capable aircraft, and further comprises a delivery mechanism configured to extend payload below and exterior to the vertical flight capable aircraft for delivery fore or aft of the aircraft. 26. The vertical flight capable aircraft of cla one of claims 1 to 25, further comprising a payload bay door positioned to provide access to the payload bay, and further comprising a removable reinforcing structure coupled to at least a portion of the payload bay door. 27. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 26, further comprising a missile launch system. 28. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 27, wherein the missile launch system is at least partially positioned within the payload bay. 29. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 27, wherein the missile launch system is selected from the group consisting of a JAGM rail system, a LAU-61 system, and a LAU-131 system. 30. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 29, further comprising a payload release system. 31. The vertical flight capable aircraft of one of claims 1 to 30, further comprising a UAV release system. 32. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 31, wherein at least a portion of the UAV release system is positioned within the payload bay. 33. The vertical flight capable aircraft of claim 31, wherein the UAV release system comprises a common launch tube.
